name: Mom's German Meatballs
recipeInstructions: This is a bit of a task to prepare, but well worth the effort. The recipe was passed down for many generations in my husband's family who immigrated here from Russia (being Russian Jews), so I have no idea why they called it 'German' meatballs, but a meatball by any other name... I guess! Put the garlic into a water glass and pour 2 tbsp boiling water over it. Mash the garlic well, then add 2 more tablespoons of boiling water. Strain through a kitchen towel, saving the liquid only. Mix together all the ingredients, including the garlic liquid, then shape into balls about the size of a large egg. Roll the balls in flour until well coated. Melt enough fat to make about 1/2 inch in a heavy skillet or cast iron cooking pot. Brown the meatballs in the fat. Remove the meatballs and pour off all but 4 tbsp of the fat. SAUCE: Add the quart of water to the reserved fat along with the catsup. Make a paste of the sauce ingredients, stirring in the water until you get a consistency of a thin cream. After the water-catsup mixture is warm, slowly add this mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumping, until the mixture has been well absorbed into the liquid. Cook gently until the sauce is slightly thickened. Place meatballs in the pan or an oven-proof roaster and make sure they are covered with the sauce. Cover pan and bake in hot oven for 1 hour. Serve with mashed potatoes (My friends also love it with pasta or rice!).
